message:
feat(core,abi): port wcstol/wcstoul wide-char parsing to core::stdlib::conversion (bd-wcv-epic)

wchar_abi.rs duplicated ~205 lines of base/sign/prefix/digit-
accumulation logic that already lived in core::stdlib::conversion::
strtol_impl / strtoul_impl, but on `&[u32]` (wchar_t) instead of
`&[u8]`. Plus three local helpers (wide_is_space, wide_digit_value,
wide_is_ascii_hexdigit) replicated stdlib utilities. Lift the wide
variants into core::stdlib::conversion alongside the byte versions,
sharing the existing ConversionStatus enum.

New core surface (in stdlib::conversion):
  pub fn wide_is_space(wc: u32) -> bool       // Unicode-aware
  pub fn wide_digit_value(wc: u32) -> Option<u32>
  pub fn wcstol_impl(s: &[u32], base: i32) -> (i64, usize, ConversionStatus)
  pub fn wcstoul_impl(s: &[u32], base: i32) -> (u64, usize, ConversionStatus)

Logic is structurally identical to strtol_impl / strtoul_impl: leading
whitespace skip, optional +/- sign, base==0 auto-detect (0x→16, 0→8,
else 10), base==16 + 0x prefix consumed, base in 2..=36 validation,
accumulator overflow handling (saturate to MAX/MIN with Overflow/
Underflow status while continuing to consume digits to advance the
cursor).

abi changes:
  - Drop wide_is_space, wide_digit_value, wide_is_ascii_hexdigit,
    parse_wide_signed, parse_wide_unsigned.
  - wcstol shim calls core::stdlib::conversion::wcstol_impl directly.
  - wcstoul shim calls wcstoul_impl directly.

Verified via rch (CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cc_bd-wcv-*):
  - 31 new core tests (helpers + signed + unsigned + 3 byte-vs-wide
    parity tests across base 0/8/10/16/36 and i64::MAX/MIN and u64::MAX)
  - 173/173 abi lib tests pass
  - 107/107 wchar_abi + conformance_diff_wchar integration tests pass
  - cargo clippy -D warnings clean
  - cargo fmt --check clean

Closes bd-wcv-epic.
